data.title: check-usage.sh reports erroneous usage values causing false VERDICT: 2 (paused) throttle
data.description:
## Observed Behavior
check-usage.sh reports:
- USAGE_5HR: 1600%
- USAGE_7DAY: 7000%
- VERDICT: 2 (paused — all dispatch halted)

## Expected Behavior
Actual Claude Code session usage was approximately 21% (session) and 70% (week), which should produce VERDICT: 0 (unlimited) and not block sub-agent dispatch.

## Impact
Sprint batch execution incorrectly halted. User had to manually override the gate. All sub-agent dispatch was blocked despite available capacity.

## Reproduction
Run: `bash plugins/dso/scripts/check-usage.sh`
Compare reported percentages against actual usage shown in Claude Code status or session info.

## Hypothesis
The percentage calculation in check-usage.sh may be dividing by an incorrect baseline (wrong token limit, mismatched units, or stale/cached denominator), causing wildly inflated percentage values that always trigger VERDICT: 2.
